title_en: The Story of DOGE, as Told by Federal Workers
title_zh: DOGE 的故事，来自联邦员工的亲历叙述
rubric_en: WIRED spoke with more than 200 federal workers in dozens of agencies to learn what happened as the Department of Government Efficiency tore through their offices.
rubric_zh: WIRED 采访了来自数十个机构的两百多名联邦工作人员，了解政府效率部门在他们办公室“清洗”期间发生了什么。

paragraphs.en: In August, months after Elon Musk left the federal government, the director of the Office of Personnel Management offered the first hard estimate of the so-called Department of Government Efficiency’s impact on the civil service. The government would likely end 2025 with about 300,000 fewer employees than it had at the start of the year, he told reporters. Most resignations were attributable to the incentives DOGE had offered the federal workforce to resign their positions. The total figure amounted to one in eight workers.
paragraphs.zh: 8 月，在伊隆·马斯克离开联邦政府数月后，人事管理局局长首次给出了所谓“政府效率部”对公务员体系影响的较为明确的估算。他告诉记者，政府到2025年底时的雇员人数可能比年初少约30万。大多数辞职都可追溯到 DOGE 提供给联邦员工的辞职激励措施。总人数相当于每八名员工就有一名。

paragraphs.en: Well, almost. In recent weeks, hundreds of the employees DOGE pushed out have reportedly been offered reinstatement.
paragraphs.zh: 更准确地说，不完全是这样。最近几周来，据报数百名被 DOGE 劝退的员工已经被提议恢复任职。

paragraphs.en: The true scope of DOGE’s attack on the federal government remains unknown. While there is no reason to think it achieved meaningful cost savings or operational efficiencies, the ramifications of building a master database to track and surveil immigrants are just beginning to be felt, and its cadre of Musk protégés and tech entrepreneurs remain embedded in agencies throughout the executive branch. The possibilities this opens up—of private takeovers of government operations, of the government embracing Silicon Valley’s ethos of moving fast and breaking things—remain open.
paragraphs.zh: DOGE 对联邦政府冲击的真实范围仍然不明。虽然没有证据表明它取得了有意义的成本节约或运作效率提升，但在建设一个用于跟踪和监控移民的主数据库之后，连锁影响才刚刚开始显现；而其麾下的马斯克门徒和科技创业者仍然嵌在行政部门各个机构之中。它所打开的可能性仍在展开：可能出现私人力量接管政府运作，可能出现政府采纳硅谷“快速行动、敢于打破常规”的治理方式。

paragraphs.en: WIRED spoke with more than 200 federal workers across dozens of agencies to gather the most comprehensive picture yet of how the American government got to this point, and where it may go from here. Many sources requested anonymity because they fear retaliation. They told WIRED not just what has been going on inside the federal government at a time of unprecedented change—but what it’s been like to experience those changes firsthand.
paragraphs.zh: WIRED 与来自数十个机构的两百多名联邦工作人员进行了访谈，拼凑出截至目前最完整的画面，以呈现美国政府如何走到今天，以及接下来可能走向何方。许多受访者要求匿名，因为他们担心遭到报复。受访者告诉 WIRED 的，不仅是美国联邦政府在前所未有变革时期内部正在发生的事情，更是亲历这些变化后的真实体验。

paragraphs.en: The following is the story, in their words, of what happened when the world’s most powerful man unleashed the world’s richest one on the world’s most complex institution.
paragraphs.zh: 以下是他们自己的说法：当世界上最有权势的人把世界上最富有的人放到世界上最复杂的机构里时，发生了什么。

paragraphs.en: “I kept comparing it to a natural disaster,” one worker at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention told WIRED. “But it wasn’t natural. Just a stampede of wide-eyed, confused government employees moving files around and looking over their shoulders because they think maybe Elon was creeping behind them with a chain saw.”
paragraphs.zh: “我一直把它比作自然灾害，”一位美国疾病控制与预防中心的员工告诉WIRED。“但这并非自然灾害。更像是一场挤得满满当当的人群踩踏——一群睁大了眼睛、困惑不已的政府员工在搬文件、回头张望，因为他们觉得也许伊隆就在后面拿着电锯悄悄跟着他们。”

paragraphs.en: Donald Trump established DOGE within hours of taking office on January 20, assigning it the task of “modernizing Federal technology and software to maximize governmental efficiency and productivity.” Within days, Musk’s allies and their coterie of young, inexperienced technologists were appearing in the offices of relatively unknown agencies like the General Services Administration and the Office of Personnel Management—obtaining unprecedented access to government systems and personnel files in the process. The DOGE operatives included young men like Edward “Big Balls” Coristine, Kyle Schutt, and Ethan Shaotran, all of whom would go on to work at a number of government agencies, from the Department of Education to the Social Security Administration.
paragraphs.zh: 唐纳德·特朗普于1月20日就职后数小时内成立了DOGE，赋予其“将联邦技术和软件现代化，以最大化政府效率和生产力”的任务。在接下来几天内，马斯克的盟友以及一批年轻、缺乏经验的技术人员便出现在如总务管理局、联邦人事管理局等相对不为人知的机构办公室，在此过程中获得了前所未有的政府系统和人员档案访问权限。DOGE的行动人员包括Edward“Big Balls” Coristine、Kyle Schutt和Ethan Shaotran等年轻男性，他们后来又在多个政府机构工作，从教育部到社会保障局都有。

paragraphs.en: “I met Kyle and Ethan on January 23, and I very briefly bumped into Coristine before anyone was talking about him. I would describe them all as giddy, excited, curious, passionate, and super interested in learning about and jumping in on this new thing. I was super excited too at first.
paragraphs.zh: “我在1月23日见到Kyle和Ethan，并且在大家还没开始谈论Coristine之前，我也一度短暂碰过他的面。我会把他们都形容为兴奋、热情、好奇、充满激情，而且非常想了解并立刻投入这件新事物。起初，我自己也非常兴奋。”

paragraphs.en: “Then the next week it felt like everything shifted and suddenly they were no longer curious or asking questions or on an adventure and instead they were just frantically running around trying to do impossible shit with no context and no flexibility and no ability to push back.
paragraphs.zh: 「然后下一周，我们感觉一切都变了。突然之间，他们不再好奇、再提问题，也不再像在一次冒险中，而是只是在疯狂地到处奔跑，试图在没有背景、没有灵活空间、也没有任何回旋余地的情况下完成不可能完成的工作。」

paragraphs.en: “I thought maybe it would turn around. But it never did.” —General Services Administration (GSA) worker
paragraphs.zh: 「我本来以为情况会转好，但事实并非如此。」——美国总务局（General Services Administration，GSA）工作人员

paragraphs.en: “The first meeting with DOGE—really the only meeting with DOGE, if I'm going to be honest—was … a virtual meeting that was 30 minutes long. In typical DOGE fashion, the government organization that we were promised unbelievable transparency on, they don't turn the cameras on, they don't tell you who they are, they don't tell you if anybody else is in the room, so you have no idea who you're talking to.” —Colin O’Brien, former head of security at the United States Institute of Peace
paragraphs.zh: 「如果我说实话，这和 DOGE 的第一次会议——也可以说是唯一一次会议——是一场 30 分钟的线上会议。按 DOGE 一贯的方式，这个本应提供令人难以置信透明度的政府组织，他们不开摄像头，不告诉你他们是谁，也不告诉你是否还有别人和你在同一房间，所以你完全不知道自己在和谁说话。」——科林·奥布赖恩（Colin O’Brien），美国和平研究所（United States Institute of Peace）前安全负责人

paragraphs.en: “We saw them immediately. They acted like new hires but a bit furtive since they were actually instructed not to share their full names with us at first.” —Technology Transformation Services worker
paragraphs.zh: 「我们一见到他们就认出了。起初他们表现得像新员工，却又有些鬼祟，因为一开始他们确实被指示不向我们提供全名。」——技术转型服务（Technology Transformation Services）工作人员

paragraphs.en: “My big aha moment came late, because for so long I was giving them the benefit of the doubt. These guys were young, and they had a job to do, and yes, they were doing it aggressively—but again, I assumed the best. But then Ethan Shaotran went on Fox News, on Jesse Watters. He just trash-talked us pretty bad, conflating things they’d found at other agencies, basically implying we were misappropriating grant money. It made my blood boil. Ethan had to know that wasn’t true. That was it for me—there is no good faith at all.” —Federal worker
paragraphs.zh: “我的‘恍然大悟’来得很晚，因为我一直在给他们机会。那些人年轻、有职责在身，而且确实很积极地在工作——但我还是默认他们是出于善意的。但后来，Ethan Shaotran 在福克斯新闻《Jesse Watters》节目里出镜了。他几乎是在公开对我们恶语相向，把其他机构发现的问题硬凑到一起，基本上暗示我们在挪用补助金。这让我气得发狂。Ethan 肯定知道那不是真的。对我来说，这下都没完蛋了——完全没有善意。” — 联邦职员

paragraphs.en: “The vibe they gave was ‘So, what is it that you do here?’ and ‘Why can’t AI do that?’” —Technology Transformation Services worker
paragraphs.zh: “他们给人的感觉就是：‘那么，你们在这里到底做什么？’以及‘为什么不能让人工智能来做这件事？’” — 科技转型服务署工作人员

paragraphs.en: During the early weeks of the administration, emails from DOGE started showing up in federal workers’ inboxes—or at least in their spam folders.
paragraphs.zh: 在新政府初期，来自DOGE的电子邮件开始出现在联邦工作人员的收件箱里——或者至少出现在他们的垃圾邮件文件夹里。

paragraphs.en: “I logged on to find several emails tagged ‘External,’ because DOGE just brought in their own servers and plugged them into the network. Then there were several subsequent emails from different leaders saying things like, ‘Thank you for all the phishing reports, but the emails are real and need to be followed. But also please keep reporting things that look like phishing. Except from DOGE … but probably even then. And this is totally fine and normal.’” —Contractor for the Veterans Administration
paragraphs.zh: “我一登入系统，看到有几封邮件被标记为‘外部邮件’，因为DOGE刚刚接入了自己的服务器，并把它们接进了网络。后来又有多位负责人发来几封后续邮件，内容是：‘感谢你们提交了所有钓鱼邮件举报，但这些邮件属实，必须跟进。同时请继续举报那些看起来像钓鱼邮件的内容。除了来自DOGE的邮件……但大概也要举报。并且这一切都完全正常。’” — 退伍军人事务部承包商

paragraphs.en: Among the emails was the now infamous “Fork in the Road,” encouraging workers to be loyal or quit. It closely tracked the language of an email sent to Twitter employees not long after Musk’s takeover of that company. A subsequent follow-up was even more insulting.
paragraphs.zh: 在这些邮件中，有一封如今臭名昭著的《路口抉择》（“Fork in the Road”），鼓励员工“要么忠诚，要么离职”。它几乎逐字复刻了马斯克接管该公司后不久发给推特员工的一封邮件。后续的一封跟进邮件更是侮辱性十足。

paragraphs.en: “It was truly so idiotic and looked like it was written by a disturbed child.” —CDC employee
paragraphs.zh: “这实在太愚蠢了，看起来像是一个精神失常的孩子写的。”——疾控中心（CDC）员工

paragraphs.en: “We’re used to every little thing done by regulation, and now we’re just getting crazy emails … This is a 5-alarm fire. This is a constitutional crisis.” —Department of Labor employee
paragraphs.zh: “我们习惯了所有小事都按规定来处理，而现在却只收到这种疯狂的邮件……这简直是‘五级警报’级别的火情。这是场宪政危机。”——劳工部（Department of Labor）员工

paragraphs.en: “That response shocked me. We knew that this administration had little regard for professionals who choose to work for the federal government, but to state it so publicly, dripping with contempt, was truly unbelievable to me.” —GSA employee
paragraphs.zh: “那个回应把我震住了。我们知道这一届政府对选择为联邦政府工作的专业人士并不怎么尊重，但它能如此公开、满是轻蔑地说出来，真让我难以置信。”——总务管理局（GSA）员工

paragraphs.en: Federal employees who remained were forced back to the office. Many found their workplaces increasingly hostile.
paragraphs.zh: 留下来的联邦雇员被迫返回办公室。许多人发现自己的工作场所变得越来越不友好。

paragraphs.en: “A woman I did not know in the cubicle next to me broke down. She was literally wailing, inconsolable, because she could not get into a childcare facility she could afford on such short notice. She literally had to choose between her little child and working. Her explaining to her manager the way her child cried and begged Mommy to stay home broke me. Then, as if on cue, an email from a person whose account said they were the acting IRS commissioner arrived in our inboxes, reminding us that it was “Mental Health Awareness” month and that we can do such helpful things as “practicing gratitude” and breathing techniques to deal with stress. It also reminded us we can take time off to seek professional help … I never saw her again, and her cube is now empty.” —Internal Revenue Service employee
paragraphs.zh: “我隔壁工位有一位素未谋面的女士突然崩溃了。她几乎是彻底放声大哭、难以安抚，因为她无法在这么短时间内找到一处自己负担得起的托儿所。她真的不得不在照顾自己的小孩和继续上班之间做选择。她向经理解释时说，孩子在哭喊，恳求妈妈留在家里，这一幕让我很受打击。随后，就像有人预先安排好似的，一封邮件突然出现在我们收件箱里，发件人账号显示是代理国税局局长，邮件提醒我们这是“心理健康意识月”，并且可以通过“感恩练习”和呼吸训练等有用方法来应对压力。它还提醒我们可以请假去寻求专业帮助……我再也没见到她了，而她的工位现在空荡荡的。”——美国国内税务署（Internal Revenue Service）雇员

paragraphs.en: “Because we are part of Homeland Security, there’s always an armed guard at FEMA facilities. That’s a very standard thing. But the guys we’re used to seeing are like the contracted-out, office patrol guys—they’re mall cops, to be polite about it. They sit at the desk and make sure you have ID, and that’s the extent of their policing. We have a pretty good relationship with our local guy at the front desk of our building. He’s a nice guy; he’ll walk around our office sometimes. We share our snacks with him.
paragraphs.zh: “因为我们隶属于国土安全部，FEMA 的设施里总会有武装警卫。这是很正常的事情。但我们习惯看到的那些人通常是外包来的办公室巡逻员——如果要婉转说，就是‘商场警卫’。他们坐在前台，只负责确认你的身份证件，这就已经是他们全部的巡逻内容。我们和大楼前台的本地警员关系挺好。人很不错；他有时会在我们办公室里走动。我们会和他分享零食。”

paragraphs.en: “One day he comes in to walk the office, like he does occasionally, and a few minutes after he passes by, another officer walks through. This time, it’s somebody we’ve never seen before, and he’s like, kitted out. He’s dressed in all black from head to toe; he’s got body armor on. He’s wearing a tactical helmet. He’s got a big gun on him, like a rifle, not like a handgun in a side pouch. He did not have a visible name tag, which is not standard at all—everybody in a federal facility is required to identify themselves at all times, and he had no individual identifying markings. The only writing on his uniform was the big yellow text on his back that said HOMELAND SECURITY.
paragraphs.zh: “有一天他过来像平时偶尔那样巡查办公室，几分钟后刚他经过不久，又进来一名警官。这次是个我们从未见过的人，而且他装备齐全。

paragraphs.en: “He was walking through the office as slowly as he could. He would pause behind you and watch what you were doing for five to 20 seconds and then move on. His entire demeanor, it was very obvious he wanted us to see him, and he wanted to see us watching him. These are fucking office workers working on laptops. We’re doing spreadsheets and PowerPoints. Like, chill out, dude.
paragraphs.zh: “他走廊里走得非常慢。会在你身后停留片刻，盯着你看你正在做什么，大概五到二十秒，然后就走开。可以明显看出，他就是想让我们看到他，也想让我们都在看着他。我们这些他妈的办公室职员都在用笔记本电脑工作。我们在做电子表格和 PPT。就，放轻松点，伙计。”

paragraphs.en: “My coworker went over [to the guard] and was like ‘Hey, welcome, can I help you with something, are we in danger? What’s the problem? The officer announces—not just to my coworker but to as many people as can hear him—‘No, I don’t want your help. I’m just here to patrol you and to desensitize you to my presence.’ Then he just keeps on walking. That’s the only thing he’s said to us. He’s been through a couple times now and never gives anyone the time of day—never smiles, never says good morning, just walks through, dresses us all down for a few minutes, and leaves.” —FEMA employee
paragraphs.zh: “我的同事走到警卫面前，说‘嘿，欢迎，请问有什么我可以帮忙的吗？我们有危险吗？出了什么问题？’那名军官对着他——而且对着所有能听见的人——宣布说：‘不，我不需要你的帮助。我只是来巡查你们，让你们对我的出现习惯一下。’然后他就一直走着。对我们来说，他只说过这句。他现在已经进来过好几次了，从来没给过任何人一分钟——不笑，不说早安，只是走过去，盯我们看几分钟，然后离开。”—FEMA 员工

paragraphs.en: The offices were also quite literally gross—because DOGE had put a $1 spending limit on most government credit cards and didn’t make exceptions for basic necessities.
paragraphs.zh: 办公室的环境也确实很糟糕——因为 DOGE 对大多数政府信用卡都设了 1 美元的支出上限，而且对基本必需品也不做例外。

paragraphs.en: “The women’s restroom was out of toilet paper within a week or so of us coming back to the office. I brought this up to Facilities, like, ‘Hey, this is kind of a sanitation and dignity issue, can you hook us up with more toilet paper?’ They were like, ‘We’d love to, but we can’t purchase anything until they unfreeze the cards, and we don’t even know what the process is, because they have them sort of indefinitely frozen.’
paragraphs.zh: “我们回到办公室一周左右，女厕所里的厕纸就没了。我向设施管理部门反映说：‘这个问题关系到卫生和尊严，你们能不能再给我们补点厕纸？’他们回复：‘我们很想帮你们，但在解冻这些卡之前我们无法采购任何东西，而且我们也不知道流程是什么，因为卡已经被无限期冻结了。’”

paragraphs.en: “For five months we were instructed to bring in our own toilet paper. I literally kept two rolls at my desk. I wish I were joking.” —FEMA employee
paragraphs.zh: “过去五个月，我们被要求自带马桶纸。我桌上真的放了两卷。我真希望这只是在开玩笑。”——联邦紧急事务管理署（FEMA）员工

paragraphs.en: Musk and Trump were eagerly filling government agencies with their allies. At the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD), Trump installed Scott Turner, a former NFL cornerback.
paragraphs.zh: 马斯克和特朗普正急切地用他们的盟友填补各政府机构的空缺。在住房与城市发展部（HUD），特朗普任命了前NFL角卫斯科特·特纳。

paragraphs.en: “[Turner] has two primary sources of anecdotes I keep hearing: his time in the NFL—specifically that he was drafted in the last round—and his father’s time working as a shelf stocker at Safeway and how he is doing the same thing at HUD by ‘taking inventory and restocking the shelves.’ For a motivational speaker and pastor he is neither motivational nor inspirational.” —HUD employee
paragraphs.zh: “[特纳]有两个我反复听到的‘故事来源’：一是他在NFL的经历——特别是他在最后一轮才被选中的经历；二是他父亲曾在Safeway当过货架补货员的经历，而他在HUD也是在做同样的事，‘清点库存、给货架补货’。对一名职业演讲者和牧师来说，他既不振奋人心，也不具备鼓舞性。”——HUD员工

paragraphs.en: Immigration was a major pillar of the second Trump administration. While the government made crystal clear it did not want foreigners in the country, it carved out an exception for a minority group of white South Africans in an executive order in February—a topic near and dear to Musk’s heart, as a white immigrant from South Africa.
paragraphs.zh: 移民议题是特朗普第二任政府的核心支柱之一。尽管政府明确表明不希望外国人进入美国，却在2月的一项行政命令中为一小群白人南非裔开了例外，这在马斯克身上引起了强烈共鸣——他本身就是一位来自南非的白人移民。

paragraphs.en: “This administration has made a complete mockery of the humanitarian side of immigration. I have interviewed parents who saw their children beheaded in front of them. I have interviewed children who saw their parents killed in front of them. I have interviewed women who have been sexually assaulted. I have interviewed children who have been sexually assaulted. I have interviewed teenagers who were beaten and threatened by their own family because they were part of the LGBTQI+ community. What has happened to these people in South Africa that warrants refugee status in the US? Nothing.” —Department of Homeland Security employee
paragraphs.zh: “这个政府对移民的人道一面简直是彻底的讽刺。我曾采访过父母眼前孩子被砍头的家长。我曾采访过孩子眼前父母被杀害的孩子。我曾采访过遭受性侵的女性。我曾采访过遭受性侵的儿童。我曾采访过因为属于 LGBTQI+ 群体而被自家人殴打、威胁的青少年。是什么让这些人在南非遭遇的事情足以获得美国难民身份？答案是什么也没有。”——国土安全部（Department of Homeland Security）员工

paragraphs.en: Musk said he wanted to downsize the federal workforce. What was the right size, exactly? As lean as possible. Entire agencies were gutted as tens of thousands of federal employees were subjected to reductions in force, or RIFs. Some of these actions have been challenged in court, but the Supreme Court recently ruled that the Trump administration could continue its proposals to potentially lay off federal workers en masse.
paragraphs.zh: 马斯克说他想缩减联邦工作人员规模。到底“合适”的规模是多少？尽可能少。随着数万名联邦雇员被裁减，许多机构都被掏空，甚至出现了“裁员缩编”（RIF，reductions in force）。其中一些举措已被法院提起挑战，但最高法院最近裁定，特朗普政府可以继续推进其可能大规模裁员联邦工作人员的方案。

paragraphs.en: “The moment everything crystallized for me was the day they came for a respected career deputy. Someone who embodied integrity and competence. His ‘crime’? Having the guts to challenge DOGE’s reckless RIF plans. One afternoon, he returned from lunch to find security waiting at his desk. No explanation, no warning—just a quiet escort out of the building while stunned colleagues looked on. Years of dedicated service reduced to a public humiliation.” —Department of Labor employee
paragraphs.zh: “对我来说一切都彻底定性清楚的那一刻，是他们去找一位备受尊敬的资深副手那天。这个人是正直和胜任的化身。他的‘过错’是什么？就是敢于挑战 DOGE 那些鲁莽的裁员缩编计划。某个下午，他从午餐回来，发现有警卫在他办公桌旁等着。没有解释，没有预警——只是冷静地护送他离开楼宇，周围是震惊的同事目睹。多年的奉献一瞬间被降格为公开羞辱。”——劳工部（Department of Labor）员工

paragraphs.en: “I knew what the powers that be were doing wasn't legal. So either they were incompetent and didn't know it was illegal, or they knew it was illegal and didn’t care. Which one is scarier?” —CDC employee
paragraphs.zh: “我知道那些掌权者正在做的事不合法。那他们不是不懂这种做法违法、能力不足，就是明知违法却毫不在乎。哪个更可怕呢？”——美国疾控中心（CDC）员工

paragraphs.en: “What stands out to me is how disorganized and unprofessional the GSA reduction in force was. Staff were instructed to return government IDs ASAP. We lost Google Drive access immediately, and the agency put resources about our RIF on there. We were blocked from sending emails to non-GSA addresses. Even trying to email career documents to your private email address became a huge issue.” —GSA employee
paragraphs.zh: “让我印象最深的是，GSA的裁员行动多么混乱和不专业。员工被要求尽快归还政府工作证。我们立刻失去了对Google Drive的访问权限，而机构将有关RIF的资料放在了那里。我们还被阻止向非GSA地址发送电子邮件。甚至把就业相关文件发到你自己的私人邮箱，也一下子变成了一个大麻烦。”——GSA员工

paragraphs.en: “When the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau was first gutted, one person left their blazer in the office and was unable to get back into the building to get it. It was the only blazer they owned: They were broke, applying for jobs, and had nothing to wear to interviews because of this.” —CFPB employee
paragraphs.zh: “当消费者金融保护局刚被大规模裁员时，有人把他们的西装外套落在了办公室里，却无法再进楼去拿。那是他们唯一的一件西装外套：他们那时拮据，正在投递求职申请，这件事导致他们没有衣服去参加面试。”——CFPB员工

paragraphs.en: On February 14, tens of thousands of federal workers lost their jobs in an event that would become known to those impacted as the Valentine’s Day Massacre. Other workers were told they were going to receive firing letters imminently—only to wait days with no news.
paragraphs.zh: 2月14日，成千上万名联邦员工在一场后来被受影响者称为“情人节大屠杀”的事件中失去了工作。其他员工被告知即将收到解雇通知，但最终却被迫等待了几天却杳无音讯。

paragraphs.en: “My fiancée and I had just come back from dinner. We’re getting ready to go to bed. I decide I’m just going to disconnect from social media and my email. I’m just going to turn it off … I saw I had an unread message. I was fired at 11, 11:30 pm. [My fiancée] looks at me, and she sees my demeanor change. [She says,] ‘That was the email, wasn't it?’” —Fired Federal Aviation Administration aeronautical information specialist
paragraphs.zh: “我和未婚妻刚刚从晚餐回来。我们正准备上床。我决定先断开社交媒体和电子邮件，直接把它关掉……结果我看到一封未读信息。我在晚上11点到11点半之间被解雇了。[未婚妻]看着我，看到我的情绪变了。[她说：]‘这就是那封邮件吧？’”——被解雇的联邦航空局航空情报专家

paragraphs.en: “It was Valentine’s Day, and my partner planned a romantic dinner for us that I ate in a catatonic state, in my sweatpants, covered in tears.” —CDC employee
paragraphs.zh: “那是情人节，我的伴侣为我们安排了浪漫晚餐，但我穿着运动裤、满脸泪水、像麻木发呆一样吃下了那顿饭。”——美国疾病控制与预防中心（CDC）员工

paragraphs.en: On February 22, in another echo of his Twitter takeover, Musk warned that the entire federal workforce needed to write an email explaining what they’d gotten done the previous week.
paragraphs.zh: 2 月 22 日，在他另一次社交媒体接管式作风的回响中，马斯克警告说，整个联邦劳动力都需要写一封电子邮件，说明他们在上一周完成了什么工作。

paragraphs.en: “It was so humiliating to have to prove, ostensibly to Elon Musk—someone not in my chain of command or even a government employee—what I was doing. Not only is it none of their business what I was up to (they are not my supervisor), but they wouldn’t even understand anything I put in there anyway since it’s far too technical. I put read receipts on my first submission, and after I hadn’t gotten pinged that it had been read after two subsequent submissions, I just stopped sending them. It made me so mad that not only are they passive-aggressively insinuating I’m doing nothing, but they’re wasting tons of federal workers’ time (and taxpayer money) doing this exercise, and they aren’t even opening the emails. Infuriating.” —Department of Defense employee
paragraphs.zh: “要向伊隆·马斯克——一个不在我汇报链条里、甚至不是政府雇员的人——证明我在做什么，这太羞辱人了。更何况他们根本不该管我在做什么（他们不是我的主管），而且他们其实也不可能理解我写的内容，因为太专业、太技术化了。我在第一次提交时加了已读回执，在我连续提交了两次后仍没有看到对方已读提示，于是就不再继续发送了。他们让我非常生气：不仅是在被动地暗示我在摸鱼，反而还让成千上万名联邦员工（以及纳税人的钱）在这项“练习”上浪费了大量时间，但他们连邮件都不打开。真是令人恼火。”——美国国防部（Department of Defense）员工

paragraphs.en: “[Employees were responding with] emails in different languages … responding with the Constitution, and (for someone coming right back from maternity leave) responding with things such as: ‘breastfed a newborn for X number of hours, changed Y number of diapers with Z throughput, managed stakeholder input from my in-laws on best ways to burp a child.’” —VA IT worker
paragraphs.zh: “[员工们在回复时]用不同语言写邮件……写上宪法条文，而且（对于刚从产假刚回来的员工来说）还会写上诸如：‘给新生儿母乳喂养了X小时，换了Y片尿布、效率为Z，处理了父母对婴儿拍嗝最佳方法的意见。’”——退伍军人事务部（VA）IT员工

paragraphs.en: “I actually laughed pretty hard [at Musk’s email]. It’s just so ridiculous … It’s either [that or] be mad 24/7 (which some of my compatriots have decided to do), and I just don’t have the energy anymore.” —FAA air traffic controller
paragraphs.zh: “我其实对[马斯克的邮件]笑得很厉害。它太荒唐了……要么是[那么做]，要么就24/7生气（而我的一些同胞已经决定这么做了），而我早就没那个精力了。”——FAA空中交通管制员

paragraphs.en: On March 14, 2025, Colin O’Brien, then the head of security for the United States Institute of Peace, learned that the agency’s board had purportedly been fired. DOGE associates, including one named Nate Cavanaugh, arrived at USIP headquarters in Washington, DC.
paragraphs.zh: 2025年3月14日，当时担任美国和平研究所安全负责人科林·奥布莱恩（Colin O’Brien）得知该机构理事会据称已被解雇。DOGE的人员到达了华盛顿特区的美国和平研究所总部，其中一名名叫内特·卡瓦汉（Nate Cavanaugh）的人也在其中。

paragraphs.en: “The on-duty security lieutenant called me and said, ‘Hey, DOGE is here.’ The instructions we had given were that any visit by DOGE that was unscheduled, they were not to be permitted entry. If they had a scheduled appointment, absolutely, we’d have let them in. They stayed outside for a little bit less than 30 minutes and then left.”
paragraphs.zh: “当班的安全中尉打电话给我说：‘嘿，DOGE来了。’我们当时收到的指示是，如果DOGE的来访不是预先安排的，就不允许他们进门；如果他们有预约，我们当然会让他们进去。他们在外面待了不到30分钟，然后就离开了。”

paragraphs.en: Later, O’Brien got another call from the front security desk.
paragraphs.zh: 后来，奥布莱恩又接到前台值班台的一个电话。

paragraphs.en: “They're like, ‘Hey, the FBI is here with DOGE.’ So we step outside. It was two FBI agents to our right and then four DOGE people to the left, sort of standing in a semicircle. They're dressed like college kids—sneakers and jeans that are too tight—certainly not business attire.
paragraphs.zh: “他们说：‘喂，FBI来了，和DOGE一起来的。’于是我们都走到外面。那时我们右边站着两名FBI特工，左边站着四名DOGE人员，大概排成一个半圆形。看上去他们穿得像大学生——球鞋和太紧的牛仔裤——完全不算商务装束。”

paragraphs.en: “Our attorney asked the FBI: Why are you guys here? Do you have a court order, a warrant, anything? And they said no, and they said, ‘We're here to facilitate a cordial conversation.’ They looked embarrassed to be there, being just very honest.
paragraphs.zh: “我们的律师问FBI：‘你们为什么来这里？有没有法庭命令、搜查令什么的吗？’他们回答说没有，并说‘我们是来促进一次礼貌交流的。’他们看起来很尴尬，明显就是太过坦率了。”

paragraphs.en: “The conversation lasts maybe five minutes, then the DOGE people leave with the FBI agents. I didn’t realize at the time that one of the women in the DOGE SUV had run around to the side of the building and was trying to convince one of the guards to let her in through a side door, claiming at first that it was cold and she had to go to the bathroom. Then that changed, when she was told no, to: ‘I have every right to be in a government building.’ So on one hand you have the intimidation factor of federal agents plus DOGE at your front door. And then you have this juvenile covert attempt in the back door. If she’d gotten in the door she would’ve probably called 911 and claimed false imprisonment or something.”
paragraphs.zh: “谈话大约持续了五分钟，随后DOGE的人和FBI特工一起离开。我当时没意识到，车里那位DOGE女士之一已经绕到大楼侧面，试图说服一名保安让她从侧门进来。起初她说外面太冷，她得去洗手间；但被拒绝后，她又改口说：‘我有权进入政府大楼。’也就是说，你一边面对的是联邦特工加上DOGE在你家门口带来的威慑感；一边又有这种孩子气的、偷偷摸摸的侧门尝试。如果她进得去门，估计就会打911并谎报“非法拘留”之类的事情。”

paragraphs.en: DOGE ultimately got into the building (estimated value: $500 million; DOGE attempted to gift GSA the space but was blocked by a federal judge) and fired nearly every employee. On March 28, as staff were receiving termination notices—and finding out their health care would be cut the same day—one of O’Brien’s colleagues spied a salad in the office refrigerator with the name “Nate C” on it and promptly threw it in the trash.
paragraphs.zh: DOGE最终进入了该建筑（估值约5亿美元；DOGE曾试图将该空间赠与GSA，但被一名联邦法官阻止），并解雇了几乎所有员工。3月28日，当员工们正在收到解雇通知并得知他们的医疗保险当日即被取消时，O’Brien的一位同事在办公室冰箱里发现了一个写着“ Nate C”的沙拉，并立即把它扔进了垃圾桶。”

paragraphs.en: Elsewhere, DOGE was entering more agencies for the first time. Departments that rarely faced political strain, such as those that support arts and culture programs, were put under a microscope. Others, like the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, were put on ice.
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，DOGE第一次进驻更多机构。那些很少承受政治压力的部门，例如支持艺术与文化项目的部门，也被摆在显微镜下审视。其他机构，比如消费者金融保护局（CFPB），则被按下了“暂停键”。

paragraphs.en: “No one has ever been against museums and libraries before. It seems like a really nonpolitical issue, and here we are facing a hostile takeover in real time … We were put on leave on March 31. By April 3, we were already starting to hear about grants being terminated. They were done through email, very generically, not through the official recordkeeping system, saying that they no longer met the priorities of the agencies. We initially heard about it from grantees. They were posting things publicly. On LinkedIn. On social media.” —Institute of Museum and Library Services employee
paragraphs.zh: “以前几乎没人会反对博物馆和图书馆。看起来这是一个非常非政治化的问题，但我们却亲眼看到一场实时的敌意接管……我们在3月31日被安排休假；到4月3日，关于补助金被终止的消息就开始出现。通知通过电子邮件发出，极其泛泛，并没有通过正式的档案留存系统，内容是这些项目不再符合机构的优先事项。我们最初是从受资助方那里听到的，他们把消息公开发布了。比如在LinkedIn上，在社交媒体上。”——国家博物馆与图书馆服务协会员工

paragraphs.en: “People’s livelihoods were gone, wiped out and thoroughly decimated. So many of these grantees work their entire lives to see a project, book, or exhibit come to fruition. These dreams came to a crashing and fiery end in the single hit of a button by someone who likely would never understand the true devastating impact.” —National Endowment for the Humanities employee
paragraphs.zh: “大家的生计消失了，遭到彻底毁灭。太多受资助者一生都在努力，把一个项目、一部书、或一个展览实现。现在，这些梦想在一位按下按钮的瞬间，像坠崖般突然以火一般的方式结束，而且那人很可能从未真正理解其深重的破坏性后果。”——美国国家人文学科基金会（NEH）员工

paragraphs.en: “I like to think of it as I am being slightly defiant. I’ll say, ‘Although I was hired to do [certain duties] under the law, I have not been permitted to do work, so my accomplishments are limited to submitting a timesheet.’” —CFPB employee
paragraphs.zh: “我觉得自己有点带着挑衅的意味来表达：‘虽然我是根据法律被雇来做[某些职责]的，但我没有被允许开展工作，所以我的工作成果只局限于提交一份时间表。’”——消费者金融保护局（CFPB）员工

paragraphs.en: After an initial flurry of lawsuits related to DOGE access to sensitive networks, the president decided to unilaterally declare on March 20 that they could go, essentially, wherever they wanted.
paragraphs.zh: 在最初围绕 DOGE 进入敏感网络的诉讼稍有缓和后，总统在3月20日单方面宣布，他们可以进出机关办公区，基本上到哪里都可以。

paragraphs.en: “Learning about that was like getting punched in the stomach. It goes against everything our agency stands for, our mission. I have personally encouraged immigrant parents to file for benefits for their American children because those children needed it, and have promised all of them that they were safe and that we didn’t share their information, and they trusted me. And now this administration has made a liar out of me.” —Social Security Administration employee
paragraphs.zh: “得知此事就像被人重重一拳打在胃上。这与我们机构所坚持的一切、我们的使命完全相悖。我曾亲自鼓励移民家长为他们的美国孩子申请福利，因为这些孩子确实需要，而且我向他们每个人保证他们是安全的，也没有泄露他们的信息，他们也信任我。而如今，这个政府让我成了骗子。”——美国社会保障局员工

paragraphs.en: At some agencies, that access extended to taking control of official social media accounts.
paragraphs.zh: 在一些机构，这种权限甚至延伸到了对官方社交媒体账户的接管。

paragraphs.en: “I had to turn over access to our website and social media accounts. Ethan Shaotran asked me to turn over the login info to the website, to Facebook, Instagram, and X. I gave the passwords and usernames for those accounts. He came back later and asked for the address to log in to the WordPress account. I tried to just give the exact information they asked for because I wanted to passively resist. That’s why DOGE didn’t get access to our LinkedIn—they didn’t ask for it. The public considers these guys to be tech geniuses, but I’d say WordPress is pretty intuitive. It took them two days to take the website down.” —Federal worker
paragraphs.zh: “我不得不交出我们的网站和社交媒体账户权限。伊森·肖特兰（Ethan Shaotran）让我把网站、Facebook、Instagram 和 X 的登录信息交给他。我把这些账户的密码和用户名都提供了。后来他又回来要我提供登录 WordPress 账户的地址。我尽量只提供他们索要的准确信息，因为我想采取消极抵制。也正因为这样，DOGE 没能拿到我们 LinkedIn 的权限——他们从未索要。公众认为这些人是技术天才，但我觉得 WordPress 其实挺直观的。他们花了两天时间把网站下线。”——联邦工作人员

paragraphs.en: In March, the Social Security Administration readied a plan ostensibly to combat identity theft. Under the plan, beneficiaries would not be able to verify their identity on the phone as they had in the past. Instead they’d have to use an online portal or show up in person. (About a week later, the SSA relented and began allowing the phone again.)
paragraphs.zh: 3月，美国社会保障局（SSA）制定了一项看似为打击身份盗窃而推出的计划。根据该计划，受益人不能再像过去那样通过电话核实身份，而必须改用在线门户网站或亲自前往办公室。约一周后，SSA改变了做法，重新允许通过电话验证。

paragraphs.en: “Some elderly beneficiaries were crying on the phone, terrified that they would lose their benefits if they couldn’t figure out how to travel to an office and show us their ID. One woman was in her nineties. Another elderly couple showed up at an SSA office to prove they were alive, but it was never clear that this was necessary—no policy guidance had been issued.
paragraphs.zh: “一些年长的受益人打电话时都在哭，害怕如果不知道如何前往办事处并向我们出示身份证，就会失去福利。一个女性已经九十多岁了。另一对老年夫妇来到SSA办公室证明自己还活着，但从未明确说明这是否必要——没有发布任何政策指引。”

paragraphs.en: “We were told to be patient and wait until guidance could be provided. At one point, we were told that we would receive instructions a week AFTER the policy implementation day.
paragraphs.zh: “我们被告知要耐心等待，直到发布指引。有一段时间，我们被告知将在该政策实施日后的一周才收到指示。”

paragraphs.en: “In the end, the administration discovered that there wasn’t a problem with fraud in our teleclaims, something that frontline staff could’ve told them from the beginning. Less than 1 percent of claims were even flagged for further investigation. If our leaders would take the time to learn how to read the records, they might know this too.” —SSA employee
paragraphs.zh: “最后，管理层发现我们电话申报中的确没有发现欺诈问题，而一线员工一开始就能告诉他们这一点。真正被进一步调查的理赔申请不足1%。如果我们的领导愿意花点时间学会怎么看记录，他们也会知道这一点。” —SSA员工

paragraphs.en: The concern for security apparently did not extend to DOGE affiliates themselves.
paragraphs.zh: 对安全的担忧显然并没有延伸到 DOGE 的关联人员自身。

paragraphs.en: “In April, I happened to come across a partial list of employees and contractors who had not completed some of their mandatory security training. I wasn't surprised to see that DOGE-affiliated names made up more than a quarter of the list. It included [Technology Transformation Services director] Thomas Shedd, [Federal Acquisition Service head] Josh Gruenbaum, Ed Coristine, Luke Farritor, and Steven Davis. —Current GSA IT contractor
paragraphs.zh: “四月时，我碰巧看到一份部分名单，上面是一些未完成强制性安全培训的雇员和承包商。我并不惊讶地看到，DOGE 关联的名字占了名单的四分之一以上。名单上有[技术转型服务局负责人]托马斯·谢德、[联邦采购服务负责人]乔什·格鲁恩鲍姆、埃德·科里斯丁、卢克·法里托和史蒂文·戴维斯。——现任 GSA IT 承包商”

paragraphs.en: At AmeriCorps, DOGE called home thousands of young volunteers working on infrastructure projects or disaster relief around the country. It was chaos—a kind of chaos repeated across many agencies as spring wore on.
paragraphs.zh: 在 AmeriCorps，DOGE 召回了全国各地在基础设施项目或灾难救援中工作的数千名青年志愿者。那是一场混乱——随着春季推进，这种混乱在许多机构中反复出现。

paragraphs.en: “DOGE arrived at our agency in early April. About a week later, on Tuesday, they shut down the National Civilian Community Corps. They wanted to bring all the members home. Members were in the literal field though, like they were in forests and on trails.
paragraphs.zh: “DOGE 于四月初来到我们机构。大约一周后，在周二，他们关闭了全国文职社区兵团。他们想把所有成员都召回家。成员们其实都在现场，比如在森林里和步道上。”

paragraphs.en: “The next day, 85 percent of us were placed on administrative leave. They had people booking flights for members to get home, and their computers were cut off while they were booking. It was chaos. People were just disappearing. On April 24, people started receiving RIF notices, except a bunch of them were addressed to the wrong person.” —Current AmeriCorp employee
paragraphs.zh: “第二天，我们当中有 85% 被安排了行政休假。有人开始订机票把大家送回家，而且他们在订票时电脑被断开了。这是场一片混乱。人们就这么一个接一个消失。到 4 月 24 日开始陆续收到裁员通知（RIF），但其中很多都寄错了人。”——现任 AmeriCorp 员工

paragraphs.en: By June, Musk appeared to officially leave DOGE. With him went some key lieutenants: Steve Davis, Musk’s right-hand man during the Twitter takeover and DOGE’s de facto leader; Nicole Hollander, Davis’ partner, who played a key role at the GSA; and Katie Miller, communications lead for DOGE and the wife of White House deputy chief of staff for policy Stephen Miller. Musk’s send-off had included a friendly press conference with Trump, but that fragile peace was shattered a few days later, when Musk went to war with Trump on X. It seemed like Musk’s ouster from government—and breakup with the president—was complete.
paragraphs.zh: 到六月，马斯克似乎正式离开了 DOGE。随他离开的还有几位关键副手：史蒂夫·戴维斯，马斯克在推特收购期间的得力助手，也是 DOGE 的实际负责人；妮可·霍兰德，一度在 GSA 担任关键角色的戴维斯搭档；以及凯蒂·米勒，DOGE 的传播负责人，也是白宫政策副幕僚长史蒂芬·米勒的妻子。马斯克的告别活动中与特朗普举行过一次友好的记者会，但这种脆弱的和平在几天后被打破，马斯克在 X 上与特朗普开战。看起来，马斯克被逐出政府、与总统决裂似乎已经完成了。

paragraphs.en: “For all the talk that Trump likes teams of rivals, he doesn’t respect people who are nasty, interpersonally … I thought the cabinet secretary fights would be the end. The president was not going to tolerate him going after his cabinet secretaries, publicly or privately … The president just didn’t want that. He’s not going to tolerate that negativity. He likes all these people. These are his people. Marco’s a decade-old friend, at least, and a former competitor [with whom] he has a special comradery who he almost picked as his vice president. Peter Navarro went to jail for the guy. Took a federal charge, was found guilty, and ate it. Scott Bessent is his treasury secretary. Stuff like that was not gonna fly for very long.” —Senior Trump official
paragraphs.zh: “虽然大家都说特朗普喜欢‘竞争对手式’团队，但他并不尊重那些在人际关系上刻薄的人……我本来以为那场和内阁秘书的冲突会是个终点。总统不会容忍他公开或私下去冲击内阁秘书……总统就是不想看到那样的消极氛围。他喜欢这些人。这些都是他的人。马科是他十年交情的朋友，还是一个曾经的竞争对手——两人有着特殊的同志情谊，马斯克几乎要把他选为自己的副总统。彼得·纳瓦罗为这个人进了牢，背上了联邦指控，被判有罪，真是自作自受。斯科特·贝森特现在是他的财政部长。那种事很快就无法长期存在。”——特朗普高级官员

paragraphs.en: The era of Musk’s DOGE, the flashy, so-called agency that garnered Fox News specials and had orchestrated the biggest upheaval the modern US government has ever faced, appeared to be over. But the next phase was just beginning, with DOGE’s operatives and its ethos occupying every corner of government. And the toll on federal employees still hasn't stopped.
paragraphs.zh: 马斯克时代的 DOGE，这个充满炫目气氛、号称能提高政府效率的机构曾在福克斯新闻专题中大肆炒作，并且操办了美国现代政府历史上最大规模的动荡，似乎已经走到尽头。但新的阶段才刚刚开始，DOGE 的行动者和其办事风格已占据了政府各个角落。联邦员工所承受的伤害，仍在持续。

paragraphs.en: “We are in purgatory—not having enough resources to do our jobs and not knowing what the vision is for the agency moving forward. Leadership by utter neglect.” —GSA employee
paragraphs.zh: “我们像被困在炼狱里——没有足够的资源完成本职工作，也不知道机构未来的发展方向是什么。领导层对我们是完全置之不理。”——GSA 员工

paragraphs.en: “I am a clinical psychologist with plenty of lifetime trauma myself, but I had never actually attended therapy or seen a psychiatrist until after the inauguration. I was so upset when I first spoke to the telehealth psychiatrist that I couldn't even speak and almost had to hang up. They said that I was not the first federal employee that they'd talked to.
paragraphs.zh: “我是一名有着很多人生创伤经历的临床心理学家，但直到就职典礼之后我才真正去看过心理治疗或精神科医生。第一次和远程医疗心理医生通话时我太激动了，甚至说不出话，差点就挂断电话了。他们说我不是他们接触过的第一个联邦雇员。”

paragraphs.en: “This was the trauma that they wanted, that they planned, that they promised us. And, for once, they did a great job of delivering.” —CDC employee
paragraphs.zh: “这就是他们想要的创伤，他们事先计划好了，也向我们许下了承诺。并且，这一次，他们确实把这件事做得非常好。”——CDC 员工

paragraphs.en: “I’m terrified for our country and what all this means for the future. I need medication to help regulate me, because [Trump is] not going away anytime soon. And since I'm in HR, I can't get away from it. I live and breathe this during all my awake hours. If I'm not at work fielding questions from terrified employees or working with another team to terminate tons of people, family and friends are calling to see if I have any inside scoop or to check on the status of my own job.” —Federal HR employee
paragraphs.zh: “我为我们的国家以及这意味着什么对未来的影响感到恐惧。我需要药物来帮我稳定情绪，因为[特朗普]不会很快离开。又因为我在人力资源部，无法离开这些事情。白天我始终在为它呼吸、为它生活。要么我在工作中为惊恐的员工接听问题电话，要么和其他团队一起处理裁员大量人员的工作；而我的家人和朋友则在打电话来问我有没有内部消息，或想确认我自己的职位状态。”——联邦人事工作人员

paragraphs.en: “I’m the type of person where, like, if you push me I’ll push back. I don’t like to live in a mindset of despair and negativity. When these people do this shit, it just lights my fire. It makes me more fucking mad. This used to be the best job I’ve ever had, the best environment I’ve ever had, the best culture I’ve ever had—and they fucking ruined it. I will never ever forget how much they ruined it. I’m like, fuck these people. They can’t get me scared. I will not give them what they want. I will not just leave. I’m going to make it as difficult as possible for these fuckheads.” —FEMA employee
paragraphs.zh: “我是那种人——你把我往下压一次，我就会往上反击一次。我不喜欢陷在绝望和消极的思维里。每当这些人干这事儿，就会点燃我。我会更他妈地愤怒。这以前是我做过的最好的工作，最好的环境，最好的文化——而他们把它全部毁掉了。我永远不会忘记他们毁掉了多少。我现在真的是，去他妈的这些人。他们拿不走我的恐惧。我不会按他们的想法来。我不会就这么离开。我会让这群混蛋尽可能地难受。”——FEMA员工

paragraphs.en: Let us know what you think about this article. Submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 告诉我们您对这篇文章的想法。请写信给编辑部：[email protected]。
